rationale: Reject implementation for this repository. The thesis does not provide internally consistent empirical evidence strong enough for deterministic adoption, and the current codebase lacks the option-native data substrate required to reproduce or operationalize the method.
rejection_reasons: Reject implementation because the displayed baseline regressions do not actually show statistically significant NBP effects, while the narrative claims significance.
Reject implementation because the thesis contains unresolved inconsistencies in volatility-regime definition, sample size accounting, and NBP variable scaling.
Reject implementation because the repository has no WRDS/CRSP/OptionMetrics integration, no option-contract schema, and no option-order-flow/open-interest feature pipeline.
Reject productionization because the paper itself describes the effect as small and short-lived, which weakens the case for a deployable alpha signal even before replication.
recommendations: Treat this paper as a research question, not an implementation candidate.
If the topic remains interesting, start with a deterministic replication project using licensed options and equity data.
Build an option-native data model before attempting any Torghut feature or strategy work.
Require reproduced tables, stable regime definitions, and explicit sample-accounting logs before reassessing viability.
Keep any follow-on work confined to offline research and walk-forward validation until replication succeeds.
follow_up_actions: Acquire or identify a rights-cleared options dataset with buyer/seller initiation and open-interest fields.
Define a contract-level options schema plus daily stock-level aggregation rules for NBP and Net OI.
Publish a deterministic replication notebook or script bundle that resolves the thesis's observation-count and scaling inconsistencies.
Re-evaluate the signal only after replicated results survive realistic cost modeling and regime segmentation.

blocked.reason: Paper evidence and current repository substrate are insufficient for deterministic implementation.
evidence: Table 2 on pages 38-40 shows positive but statistically insignificant NBP coefficients, while Sec. 7.1 on pages 50-51 claims NBP is a significant positive predictor.
Sec. 3.5 on pages 23-24 defines HighVol as VIX > 20 and implies roughly 70% high-volatility days, while Sec. 5.2 on page 32 describes a top-quartile threshold.
Repo inspection found Torghut research and simulation primitives but no WRDS/OptionMetrics/CRSP integration or option-chain/open-interest schema.
blocked.smallest_unblocker: Acquire option data rights, add an option-native data model and ingestion path, and reproduce the thesis tables with deterministic code before reconsidering implementation.
